name: Governed Retrieval Foundation
objective: Implement governed hybrid candidate discovery, canonical resolution, independent Qdrant projection delivery, rebuild and rollback operations, and public/private evaluation tooling without making semantic similarity or a derived store authoritative.
scope: strict retrieval point, query, result, generation, and evaluation contracts
deterministic symbol, test, decision, memory, and incident retrieval cards
independent fenced projector delivery, checkpoints, retries, and replay
versioned Qdrant collections, indexes, aliases, cutover, rollback, deletion, and rebuild
bounded dense and sparse candidate execution plus weighted reciprocal-rank fusion
mandatory tenant, repository, lifecycle, freshness, source-hash, and canonical resolution
Bedrock Titan adapter through the standard AWS SDK chain for non-competition profiles
bounded operator CLIs, content-free telemetry, recovery procedures, and fail-closed preflight
public synthetic evaluation and access-controlled four-baseline capture tooling
explicit transfer of private quality measurement and Radeon-local activation to Sprint 10
out_of_scope: production retrieval policy activation before private holdout evidence
AMD Track 2 local language-model and embedding deployment
private corpus bodies, labels, captured prompts, and generated vectors in public Git
context-pack assembly and agent-facing token budgeting
author: codex
independent_validator: separate immutable review of the exact public closure candidate plus protected GitHub Actions
